subject: UX & Research
title: The Cost of One Missing Feature
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Cost of One Missing Feature.

hook.text: When Instagram removed the chronological feed, users started a petition with 340,000 signatures.

buildup.text: The algorithmic feed showed popular posts first, not recent ones. Users felt they were missing friends' updates.

discovery.text: Instagram's engagement actually increased. The algorithm surfaced better content. But users felt a loss of control.

twist.text: In 2022, Instagram added 'Following' and 'Favorites' feeds. It took six years to give users back their choice.

climax.text: Removing user control, even for better metrics, creates resentment. Let users choose how they experience your product.

punchline.text: Better metrics don't mean better experience. Give users control.

quiz.question: Why did users resent Instagram's algorithmic feed despite higher engagement?
options: They lost control over what they saw and felt they were missing friends' posts
